_comment: WIN-SYS-001 -- common Open/Save/file flows. This is a composition-PINNING and
platform-DELEGATION ledger, not a claim of new Material rendering. On Windows the
Open/Save picker is the OS-owned native IFileDialog created in fpicker/source/win32,
the OS itself owns the overwrite confirmation via the FOS_OVERWRITEPROMPT flag, and the
password/version/filter custom controls are added through the OS IFileDialogCustomize
interface -- all OS-rendered, out of Material scope. The in-suite Material Save As sheet
is WIN-DLG-003's scope and is NOT touched here.
What this contract pins fail-closed against real (comment-stripped) source is exactly the
seam invisible to every other contract: (a) the win32 platform-delegation boundary (the
OS picker + OS overwrite prompt stay OS-owned and are never faked as Material); (b) the
native-vs-fallback picker-selection seam in filedlghelper.cxx (SystemFilePicker ->
OfficeFilePicker fallback); and (c) the three C++ call-site message boxes filedlghelper.cxx
raises during the save flow. Those boxes have NO .ui file, so the .ui-only
dialog-notification-policy.csv literally cannot see them -- they are classified here under
the same NotificationRouter taxonomy (decision / security / credential-adjacent) and all
stay MODAL: none is a pure informational acknowledgement eligible for the bottom-right
notification stack.
The 'cross_references' block points at the three ALREADY-registered .ui roots
(querysavedialog / sfx2 password / remotefilesdialog) in dialog-notification-policy.csv by
locator only, to confirm they keep their native-exclusion (KeepModal) policy. It does NOT
re-register them: this JSON never appends to that CSV, so the notification/registry-closure
contracts cannot double-count.
It is source evidence only: runtime_verified is false -- no native build, file-picker pixels,
or runtime interaction are claimed. Some boxes (GPG, scripting-signature) are behind
feature/config availability; the checker reads source text, so conditional reachability does
not affect the static contract.
design_reference: docs/design/08-dialogs.md (8.3.1 File-flow platform delegation and surrounding message boxes)

anchors.note: The OS IFileDialog owns the overwrite confirmation via its own flag; present for both the open and the save initialisation, so no Material overwrite prompt is drawn.
The Open picker is the OS COM IFileOpenDialog (CLSID_FileOpenDialog); platform-delegated, out of Material scope.
The Save picker is the OS COM IFileSaveDialog (CLSID_FileSaveDialog); platform-delegated, out of Material scope.
The password/version/filter/read-only custom controls are added through the OS IFileDialogCustomize interface and are OS-drawn.
The SystemFilePicker -> OfficeFilePicker fallback boundary: when the native OS picker is unavailable the in-suite OfficeFilePicker (fpicker/source/office, WIN-DLG-003 scope) is selected instead.

message_boxes.note: Weld Question/YesNo box warning that saving to the chosen format loses the macro/scripting signature. A decision prompt: it must stay modal, never route to the bottom-right stack.
Weld Warning/Ok box raised when OpenPGP encryption-key selection fails during a save. A security acknowledgement: kept modal, never routed.

Weld Warning/Ok box (with a secondary STR_PASSWORD_WARNING text) shown when the entered ODF-encryption password length is outside the safe range. A credential-adjacent acknowledgement: kept modal, never routed.

cross_references.note: The 'Save changes before closing?' destructive-save confirmation. Already registered as native-exclusion (KeepModal); pinned read-only, not re-registered.
The document-encryption password entry. Already registered as native-exclusion (KeepModal); pinned read-only, not re-registered.
The remote-files (WebDAV/CMIS) open/save picker. Already registered as native-exclusion (KeepModal); pinned read-only, not re-registered.
